extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won a flat sprint in Tortona after THOMAS Geraint and ROGLIČ Primož crashed on a wet descent, forcing GEOGHEGAN HART Tao out with injury.
tactical_patterns: MILAN Jonathan repeatedly outsprinting PEDERSEN Mads at intermediate sprints signals tactical advantage converting omnium-style positioning into points jersey lead.
Caja Rural accelerated on late uncategorized terrain attempting to distance power sprinters, tactic failed but shows team recognizing terrain nuance.
GIBBONS Ryan as sole leadout man for ACKERMANN Pascal still delivered stage win, proving dedicated single helper outperforms diluted sprint trains.
Crashes inside 3km neutralizing GC gaps incentivizes reckless descending by GC riders who know they're protected by UCI rule.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: first Giro stage win since 2019 despite minimal team support reveals form resurgence, not just leadout dependency
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: beating established sprinters while simultaneously defending points jersey shows dual capacity rare in pure fastmen
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: negative: crash exit from third overall confirms INEOS depth now critically compromised with SIVAKOV Pavel also injured
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: continued illness struggles on flat stage signals deeper form crisis than single-day issue
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: third place without dedicated leadout train at age 37 on longest Giro stage suggests legs holding better than expected
rider_signals.GIBBONS Ryan: positive: solo leadout work producing stage win demonstrates underrated lead-out capability for future sprint squad recruitment
